title: chamaemelum reuterianum
intro: Chamaemelum nobile, also known as Chamaemelum reuterianum, is a perennial plant in the daisy family, native to the Iberian Peninsula and North Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its calming and sedative properties, which are attributed to its essential oils, particularly borneol and camphor. Chamaemelum nobile has been used to treat anxiety, insomnia, and restlessness, and has also been applied topically to soothe skin irritations and promote relaxation. It is often prepared as a herbal tea, oil, or tincture, and can also be used in aromatherapy to promote a sense of calm and well-being.
